channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20117] sanity test_27X: @@@@@@ FAIL: migrate failed
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Xiao Yang <xyang@ddn.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/6db15a8d-eacc-4bc9-a297-48c4e48c68ed

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/123699 - 6.8.0-85-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/123699 - 4.18.0-553.111.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

== sanity test 27X: lfs migrate honors --overstripe-count option ========================================================== 21:06:16 (1775855176)
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
1024 bytes (1.0 kB, 1.0 KiB) copied, 0.00323246 s, 317 kB/s
error: lfs migrate: /mnt/lustre/f27X.sanity: cannot swap layout: Protocol error
sanity test_27X: @@@@@@ FAIL: migrate failed
Trace dump:
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7400:error()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh:3778:test_27X()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7776:run_one()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7839:run_one_logged()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7654:run_test()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh:3792:main()
Dumping lctl log to /autotest/autotest-2/2026-04-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_123699_70_c30318de-f4d3-4c84-a062-8349f705837d//sanity.test_27X.*.1775855178.log
CMD: trevis-153vm72.trevis.whamcloud.com,trevis-153vm73,trevis-153vm74,trevis-153vm75 /usr/sbin/lctl dk > /autotest/autotest-2/2026-04-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_123699_70_c30318de-f4d3-4c84-a062-8349f705837d//sanity.test_27X.debug_log.\$(hostname -s).1775855178.log;
dmesg > /autotest/autotest-2/2026-04-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-ubuntu_123699_70_c30318de-f4d3-4c84-a062-8349f705837d//sanity.test_27X.dmesg.\$(hostname -s).1775855178.log

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: Patch https://review.whamcloud.com/65341 ("LU-5896 mdt: do not overwrite the actual buffer size") may also be fixing this issue.

"Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65343
Subject: LU-20117 mdc: fix RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS packing
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c06c83ac48b50897535392c296c5f6cf5d88da01

Yes, I think this is caused by the https://review.whamcloud.com/64757 ("LU-5896 mdt: allow clients with old mdc_swap_layouts"):

static const struct req_msg_field *mdt_swap_layouts[] = {         
        &RMF_PTLRPC_BODY,                                         
        &RMF_MDT_BODY,                                            
        &RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS,                                        
        &RMF_CAPA1,                                               
        &RMF_CAPA2,                                               
        &RMF_DLM_REQ                                              
};                                                                
...
static int mdc_ioc_swap_layouts(struct obd_export *exp,
				struct md_op_data *op_data)
{
...
	rc = mdc_prep_elc_req(exp, req, MDS_SWAP_LAYOUTS, &cancels, count);
	if (rc) {
		ptlrpc_request_free(req);
		RETURN(rc);
	}

	mdc_swap_layouts_pack(&req->rq_pill, op_data);

	payload = req_capsule_client_get(&req->rq_pill, &RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS);
	LASSERT(payload);
#if LUSTRE_VERSION_CODE < OBD_OCD_VERSION(3, 4, 53, 0)
	/* allow client to set msl_dv1/msl_dv2 fields until compat not needed */
	req_capsule_set_size(&req->rq_pill, &RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS, RCL_CLIENT,
			     sizeof(*msl));
#endif

First we pack the the RMF_DLM_REQ field and then set the size of RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS. But RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS is first in the message and RMF_DLM_REQ is last field. So we should set the size of RMF_SWAP_LAYOUTS before packing ELC.
Otherwise, mdc_prep_elc_req() -> req_capsule_client_get(pill, &RMF_DLM_REQ) will return the wrong buffer, the field can be overwritten and then the server will compute the correct offset but the message is corrupted.

This failed 11/234=4.7% runs with master-next servers, 9/204 = 4.4% with master-next clients in the previous week. It appears that retesting the failed session allows it to pass, which is itself strange given that it is reporting a protocol error. I don't see any correlation between failing distros or architectures, though failures do seem more common with interop or rolling-upgrade-client1/2 sessions than regular test sessions (though I see failures on regular review sessions as well).

So it appears to be some kind of race condition, and based where the error is reported, it appears it may be some interaction with DLM/ELC?

Reviewing the debug logs for one of the failures (not all of them have enough MDS logs to capture the error:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/665a64ae-9c93-4a81-afbf-28fea317548f

The highlights of the client debug log shows:

00000100:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693754:0:152121:0:(client.c:1491:ptlrpc_check_status()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : ffffffffffffffb9)
00000100:00000040:1.0:1776085681.693765:0:152121:0:(lustre_net.h:2430:ptlrpc_rqphase_move()) @@@ move request phase from Interpret to Complete  req@ffff0000c15957c0 x1862359498859008/t0(0) o61->lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ffff0000c47a0800@10.240.26.194@tcp:12/10 lens 568/224 e 0 to 0 dl 1776085697 ref 2 fl Interpret:RQU/200/0 rc -71/-71 job:'lfs.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295
00000002:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693777:0:152121:0:(mdc_request.c:2291:mdc_ioc_swap_layouts()) Process leaving via out (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : 0xffffffffffffffb9)
00000002:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693789:0:152121:0:(mdc_request.c:2421:mdc_iocontrol()) Process leaving via out (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : 0xffffffffffffffb9)
00800000:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693791:0:152121:0:(lmv_obd.c:1167:lmv_iocontrol()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : ffffffffffffffb9)
00000080:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693795:0:152121:0:(file.c:3735:ll_swap_layouts()) Process leaving via putgl (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : 0xffffffffffffffb9)
00000080:00000001:1.0:1776085681.693797:0:152121:0:(file.c:4709:ll_file_ioctl()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : ffffffffffffffb9)

and tracking down XID x1862359498859008 in the MDS debug log shows that the -71=-EPROTO error is appearing during unpacking of the RPC:

00000100:00100000:0.0:1776085681.690251:0:39547:0:(service.c:2531:ptlrpc_server_handle_request()) Handling RPC req@ffff989f3507d6c0 pname:cluuid+ref:pid:xid:nid:opc:job mdt00_001:8ea97c07-5acb-47e1-851b-1014c7119dcd+484:152121:x1862359498859008:12345-10.240.25.47@tcp:61:lfs.0
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690253:0:39547:0:(tgt_handler.c:745:tgt_request_handle()) Process entered
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690262:0:39547:0:(tgt_handler.c:288:tgt_request_preprocess()) Process entered
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690263:0:39547:0:(tgt_handler.c:55:tgt_mdt_body_unpack()) Process entered
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690264:0:39547:0:(lu_object.c:842:lu_object_find_at()) Process entered
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690265:0:39547:0:(lu_object.c:881:lu_object_find_at()) Process leaving (rc=18446630407454143560 : -113666255408056 : ffff989f03393448)
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690266:0:39547:0:(tgt_handler.c:94:tgt_mdt_body_unpack()) Process leaving (rc=0 : 0 : 0)
00000020:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690267:0:39547:0:(tgt_handler.c:351:tgt_request_preprocess()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551545 : -71 : ffffffffffffffb9)
00010000:00000001:0.0:1776085681.690268:0:39547:0:(ldlm_lib.c:3355:target_send_reply()) Process entered
00010000:00000200:0.0:1776085681.690269:0:39547:0:(ldlm_lib.c:3339:target_send_reply_msg()) @@@ processing error (-71)  req@ffff989f3507d6c0 x1862359498859008/t0(0) o61->8ea97c07-5acb-47e1-851b-1014c7119dcd@10.240.25.47@tcp:287/0 lens 568/0 e 0 to 0 dl 1776085692 ref 1 fl Interpret:/200/ffffffff rc -71/-1 job:'lfs.0' uid:0 gid:0 projid:4294967295

The trace shows that tgt_mdt_body_unpack() returned 0, so the only place for -EPROTO to be returned is:

        if (flags & HAS_KEY) {
                struct ldlm_request *dlm_req;

                dlm_req = req_capsule_client_get(pill, &RMF_DLM_REQ);
                if (dlm_req != NULL) {
                        union ldlm_wire_policy_data *policy =
                                        &dlm_req->lock_desc.l_policy_data;

                        if (unlikely(dlm_req->lock_desc.l_resource.lr_type ==
                                     LDLM_IBITS &&
                                     (policy->l_inodebits.bits |
                                      policy->l_inodebits.try_bits) ==
                                                MDS_INODELOCK_NONE)) {
                                /*
                                 * Lock without inodebits makes no sense and
                                 * will oops later in ldlm. If client miss to
                                 * set such bits, do not trigger ASSERTION.
                                 *
                                 * For liblustre flock case, it maybe zero.
                                 */
                                rc = -EPROTO;
                        }

This may still relate to patch https://review.whamcloud.com/49377 ("LU-5896 mdt: allow release after blocking migrations") that I tried to fix in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/64757 ("LU-5896 mdt: allow clients with old mdc_swap_layouts") but something seems still a bit off...
